ucs.title: [LLM] Higaisa LNK-spearphish chain: cmd.exe + find.exe + mshta self-extracting from .lnk
ucs.description: Hunts Higaisa's signature initial-access tradecraft — a malicious .lnk attachment whose target line invokes cmd.exe to use find.exe (or findstr.exe) to locate a marker string inside the .lnk itself, then pipes the carved payload to mshta.exe / cscript.exe / powershell.exe for execution. This unusual self-carving trick is documented in the Zscaler, PTSecurity and Malwarebytes 2020 Higaisa write-ups (G0126) and is rarely seen outside this actor.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.process_id) as pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="cmd.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("find.exe","findstr.exe","mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","powershell.exe") (Processes.parent_process="*find*.lnk*" OR Processes.parent_process="*findstr*.lnk*" OR Processes.parent_process="*for /f*find*" OR Processes.parent_process="*mshta*find*" OR Processes.parent_process="*.lnk*mshta*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(parent_cmd,"(?i)\.lnk") AND match(parent_cmd,"(?i)(find|findstr).*\.lnk") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Higaisa LNK self-carve chain: cmd.exe spawned by explorer.exe (LNK click), executing find/findstr against a .lnk and piping to a script host
let ScriptHosts = dynamic(["mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
// Stage 1 — cmd.exe with the find-from-lnk pattern, parent typically explorer.exe (user double-click)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "cmd.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessParentFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","outlook.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(find|findstr).{1,40}\.lnk"
   or InitiatingProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)for\s+/f.{1,80}\.lnk"
// Stage 2 — child is a script host (mshta/cscript/wscript/powershell)
| where FileName in~ (ScriptHosts)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          GrandParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildBin    = FileName,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Higaisa LNK self-carve chain — Sentinel SecurityEvent (EventID 4688) variant
let ScriptHosts = dynamic(["mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName endswith "cmd.exe"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(find|findstr).{1,40}\.lnk"
     or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)for\s+/f.{1,80}\.lnk"
// Pivot to the spawned script-host child within ~5s on the same host/session
| project ParentTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentCmd = CommandLine, SubjectLogonId
| join kind=inner (
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where tolower(NewProcessName) has_any ("\\mshta.exe","\\cscript.exe","\\wscript.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe")
    | project ChildTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              ChildCmd = CommandLine, ChildBin = NewProcessName, SubjectLogonId
  ) on Computer, Account, SubjectLogonId
| where ChildTime between (ParentTime .. ParentTime + 10s)
| project ParentTime, ChildTime, Computer, Account, ParentCmd, ChildBin, ChildCmd
| order by ParentTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Higaisa LNK Self-Extracting Spearphish (find/findstr + mshta chain)
id: e8f4c7a1-h1g4-4i5a-b2c3-higaisa-lnk-001
status: experimental
description: Detects the Higaisa (G0126) signature LNK initial-access tradecraft where a .lnk target invokes cmd.exe to find/findstr a marker string within the .lnk itself, then pipes the carved payload to a script host (mshta/cscript/wscript/powershell).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0126/
  - https://www.zscaler.com/blogs/security-research/return-higaisa-apt
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2020/06/higaisa
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0126
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent_cmd:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\cmd.exe'
    ParentCommandLine|re: '(?i)(find|findstr|for\s+/f).{1,80}\.lnk'
  selection_child_bin:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
  condition: selection_parent_cmd and selection_child_bin
falsepositives:
  - Niche admin scripts that grep inside .lnk files (very rare)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Higaisa's LNK lures (documented by Zscaler, PTSecurity, Malwarebytes in 2020) are unusual in that the .lnk target line embeds a cmd.exe one-liner that uses find.exe / findstr.exe to locate a marker string INSIDE the .lnk file itself, decode the carved bytes, and feed them to mshta.exe (most often) or another script host. This combination — cmd.exe spawned from explorer/outlook/archive-tool whose c
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Higaisa persistence: schtasks creating tasks pointing to %APPDATA%\Microsoft staged payloads
ucs.description: Hunts Higaisa's documented persistence pattern: after the LNK chain detonates, the actor stages payloads (svchost.exe / SManager.exe / officeupdate.exe lookalikes) under %APPDATA%\Microsoft\* and registers a Scheduled Task via schtasks.exe pointing at that path. Tied to G0126 by the consistent %APPDATA%\Microsoft staging directory and benign-sounding task names observed in the 2020 reports.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" (Processes.process="*/create*" OR Processes.process="*-create*") (Processes.process="*AppData\\Roaming\\Microsoft*" OR Processes.process="*AppData\\Local\\Microsoft*" OR Processes.process="*\\Microsoft\\svchost.exe*" OR Processes.process="*SManager*" OR Processes.process="*officeupdate*" OR Processes.process="*\\Microsoft\\Vault\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(user,"(?i)\\$$") AND NOT match(parent,"(?i)(msiexec|TiWorker|trustedinstaller)\\.exe") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Higaisa scheduled-task persistence pointing into %APPDATA%\Microsoft staging dirs
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/create","-create")
// Higaisa-specific staging paths and binary names from Zscaler/Malwarebytes 2020 IoCs
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)appdata\\(roaming|local)\\microsoft\\"
     or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\Microsoft\\svchost.exe","SManager.exe","officeupdate","\\Microsoft\\Vault\\")
// Suspicious parent: not a normal installer
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","trustedinstaller.exe","tiworker.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          SchtasksCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Higaisa scheduled-task persistence — Sentinel 4688 variant
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\schtasks.exe"
| where CommandLine has_any ("/create","-create")
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)appdata\\(roaming|local)\\microsoft\\"
     or CommandLine has_any ("\\Microsoft\\svchost.exe","SManager.exe","officeupdate","\\Microsoft\\Vault\\")
| where ParentProcessName !endswith "msiexec.exe"
     and ParentProcessName !endswith "TrustedInstaller.exe"
     and ParentProcessName !endswith "TiWorker.exe"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Higaisa Scheduled Task Persistence to %APPDATA%\Microsoft
id: 4d2b8c11-h1g4-4ab2-9f4c-higaisa-sch-002
status: experimental
description: Detects schtasks.exe creating a task whose action targets %APPDATA%\Microsoft\ staging directories or filenames (svchost.exe under Microsoft folder, SManager.exe, officeupdate.exe) consistent with Higaisa (G0126) post-exploitation persistence.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0126/
  - https://www.zscaler.com/blogs/security-research/return-higaisa-apt
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1053.005
  - attack.t1036.004
  - attack.g0126
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_schtasks:
    Image|endswith: '\schtasks.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '/create'
      - '-create'
  selection_higaisa_path:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)appdata\\(roaming|local)\\microsoft\\'
  selection_higaisa_names:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '\Microsoft\svchost.exe'
      - 'SManager.exe'
      - 'officeupdate'
      - '\Microsoft\Vault\'
  filter_legit_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
      - '\TiWorker.exe'
  condition: selection_schtasks and (selection_higaisa_path or selection_higaisa_names) and not filter_legit_parent
falsepositives:
  - Custom in-house deployment scripts that drop to %APPDATA%\Microsoft (rare — usually Program Files / ProgramData)
level: high
ucs.rationale: The combination of (a) schtasks.exe /create, (b) the action path being %APPDATA%\Microsoft (a folder Microsoft installers themselves never use — legitimate installers go to %ProgramFiles% or %ProgramData%), and (c) the specific masqueraded filenames svchost.exe, SManager.exe and officeupdate.exe published as IoCs in the Zscaler 2020 Higaisa report, makes this a tight fit for G0126's post-LNK persi
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
